reason: The 3.9.0 glossary defines a job exactly as the launching of a specific task with specific keyword-argument values.
Discussions state Procrastinate uses SELECT FOR UPDATE row locking so one worker owns execution of a fetched job row.
Docs describe LISTEN/NOTIFY wakeups plus polling intervals as a fallback path when notifications are missed.
The documented state machine includes todo/doing/succeeded/failed/aborted, todo->cancelled, and retryable failures returning to todo.
PyPI 3.9.0 metadata sets Requires-Python >=3.10 and the package description states PostgreSQL 13+.
Connector docs list five connectors, identify PsycopgConnector/SyncPsycopgConnector as psycopg3 variants, and state workers need async connectors.
Connector docs explicitly support conninfo DSN, keyword args, or libpq environment variables (PGHOST/PGUSER/etc.).
Quickstart explicitly marks export PYTHONPATH=. as required for procrastinate CLI module loading in the local tutorial setup.
Command-line docs show PROCRASTINATE_APP as the environment-variable alternative to passing --app.
Top-level-app docs warn __main__ imports can create two App instances; source task naming is module-derived dotted paths.
Tasks docs explicitly say each sync task runs in its own thread and all async tasks run in one event loop.
Task.defer_async delegates to JobDeferrer.defer_async, which returns job.id as an integer.
Batch-defer docs warn same queueing lock raises AlreadyEnqueued and fully rolls back the batch transaction.
Docs state defer serializes args to JSON before DB insert; source path uses psycopg JSON adaptation (default json.dumps), so date fails at defer.
Namespacing uses colon separator via add_namespace(name, namespace) -> "namespace:name", preserving the original dotted task name after the colon.
Worker defaults concurrency to 1 and processes parallel jobs as asyncio sub-workers/tasks on a single event loop.
FETCH_JOB_POLLING_INTERVAL is defined as 5.0 seconds in 3.9.0 worker source.
Source shows LISTEN uses a standalone connection (outside pool), pool args are passed through (no min_size override), and listen_notify=False skips listener task.
name is not logs-only: run_worker_async docs say it is also passed into JobContext, though procrastinate_workers stores only id/heartbeat.
Worker docs and source agree run_worker manages loop/open/close lifecycle, while run_worker_async is used with an already-open app.
